title: Search for the best subset of size k that maximises TURF reach
summary: Search for the best subset of size k that maximizes TURF reach against per-respondent utilities. Picks an exhaustive, sampled, or beam search method automatically and writes the top scoring sets and the best record to out_dir.
description: Reads a per-respondent utilities CSV and searches for the subset of size `k` that maximises TURF reach under the chosen scoring rule. Three search strategies are available — exhaustive, sampled (with screening), and beam — and `auto` (the default) picks one based on problem size. Writes the top-N candidates, the single best subset record, and a snapshot of the prepared input to `out_dir`.
usage:
[["maxdiff-turf::search-k"]]
utilities = "<utilities.csv>"
out_dir = "<dir>"
k = <int>
# optional:
subgroups = "<subgroups.csv>"
labels = "<labels.csv>"
include = "1,4"
exclude = "2,3"
utility_cols = "3-12"
non_utility_cols = "1,2"
forbidden_pairs = "1:2,3:4"
reach_method = "weighted-by-probability"
number_of_alt = 5
threshold = 0.8
search_method = "auto"
exhaustive_limit = 100000
top_n = 100
seed = 1
sample_factor = 20
sample_replicates = 3
beam_width = 1000
local_improve = true

arguments.name: utilities
arguments.description: Path to a per-respondent utilities CSV. One row per respondent, one column per item (after `non_utility_cols` are stripped). Numeric values are the per-respondent partworths the reach calculation operates on.

arguments.name: out_dir
arguments.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist. Receives the top-N candidates CSV, a single-row best-record CSV, and a snapshot of the prepared input.

arguments.name: k
arguments.description: Subset size to search for. Must be at least 1 and at most the number of eligible items after include / exclude filtering. The locked-in count from `include` is subtracted from `k` internally — i.e., you pass the *total* desired subset size, not the number of free slots.

arguments.name: subgroups
arguments.description: Path to an optional subgroup-membership CSV (one row per respondent, one column per subgroup). When supplied, per-subgroup reach is reported alongside the overall reach in the output. Combined with `target_subgroup` to optimise against a single subgroup.

arguments.name: labels
arguments.description: Path to an optional item-labels CSV (one row per item) used to render candidate subsets in human-readable form in the output.

arguments.name: include
arguments.description: Comma-separated 1-based item indices to force-include in every candidate. The search then only chooses the remaining `k - |include|` items from the eligible pool. Use this to lock in items that are mandatory for business reasons.

arguments.name: exclude
arguments.description: Comma-separated 1-based item indices to exclude from all candidates. Excluded items are removed from the eligible pool entirely before search begins.

arguments.name: utility_cols
arguments.description: Explicit utility column spec, 1-based, supports ranges like `3-12` and comma lists like `3,5,7`. Use when the utility columns are not contiguous or when the file has extra columns auto-detection should ignore.

arguments.name: non_utility_cols
arguments.description: Comma-separated 1-based columns to ignore (id, weight, demographic columns). Mutually informative with `utility_cols`.

arguments.name: forbidden_pairs
arguments.description: Forbidden-pair spec, e.g. `1:2,3:4`, listing 1-based item-index pairs that may not coexist in the same candidate subset. Useful for cannibalisation constraints (e.g., 'don't ship the regular and the premium variant together').

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether the input CSVs have header rows. Default `true`. Set to `false` for raw numeric files without column names.

arguments.name: reach_method
arguments.description: Reach scoring rule. `weighted-by-probability` (default) weights each respondent's contribution by the softmax probability of picking an item from the candidate within an implied task of `number_of_alt` alternatives. `first-choice` counts a respondent as 'reached' iff their highest-utility item in the subset would be the first choice across the implied task. `threshold` uses a fixed utility cutoff.

arguments.name: number_of_alt
arguments.description: Number of alternatives in the implied choice task used by the reach calculation. Default `5` matches a common best-worst task layout. Larger values dilute each item's reach contribution.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold value used by the threshold reach method. Ignored unless `reach_method = "threshold"`.

arguments.name: search_method
arguments.description: Search strategy. `auto` (default) picks based on the size of the problem: exhaustive when `C(n, k)` fits under `exhaustive_limit`, sampled or beam otherwise. `exhaustive` enumerates all candidates (only feasible for small problems). `sampled` uses screening + random sampling with replicates and is the recommended fallback for large problems. `beam` uses a deterministic beam search and is useful when reproducibility matters more than coverage.

arguments.name: exhaustive_limit
arguments.description: Maximum number of subsets enumerated by exhaustive search. If `C(n, k)` exceeds this, `auto` falls back to a non-exhaustive method. Raise it only if you have a small problem and want to force exhaustive enumeration despite the default cap.

arguments.name: top_n
arguments.description: Number of top scoring subsets to keep in the output. The single best is always written to a separate one-row file as well; `top_n` controls how many runners-up the multi-row file holds.

arguments.name: seed
arguments.description: Random seed for sampled and beam searches. Omit for non-deterministic runs (a fresh seed is drawn each call). Provide an integer for reproducibility.

arguments.name: sample_n
arguments.description: Sampled-search budget — the absolute number of candidates to evaluate. When set, overrides `sample_factor`. Use this when you need a deterministic compute budget rather than one scaled to problem size.

arguments.name: sample_factor
arguments.description: Multiplier on the auto-sampled budget when `sample_n` is unset. Higher values cover more candidates at proportional compute cost. Default `20` is a sensible middle ground; raise to ~50 for hard problems, lower to ~5 for quick exploratory runs.

arguments.name: sample_replicates
arguments.description: Number of independent sampled-search replicates to run. Replicates use different RNG streams from `seed` and are unioned at the end. Three is enough to smooth out single-run variance on most problems.

arguments.name: drop_frac
arguments.description: Fraction of items dropped each screening pass during sampled search. The screening loop iteratively prunes the eligible pool down by this fraction, scoring the survivors more thoroughly each round. Default `0.25` means a 25% pool shrink per round.

arguments.name: min_survivors
arguments.description: Minimum number of items retained during screening rounds. Floor on the screening loop's pruning so it doesn't shrink the candidate pool below a usable size.

arguments.name: screen_metric
arguments.description: Scoring rule used during sampled-search screening rounds. `hybrid` (default) blends mean and top-rate signals. `mean` uses pure mean reach. `top-rate` uses top-alpha tail concentration. `marginal` uses marginal reach contribution.

arguments.name: top_alpha
arguments.description: Top-alpha tail probability for the `top-rate` and `hybrid` screening metrics. Default `0.10` (top 10%).

arguments.name: beam_width
arguments.description: Beam search width — the number of partial candidates retained at each level of the beam. Larger widths give better coverage at proportional compute cost.

arguments.name: local_improve
arguments.description: When `true`, apply a local-improvement (greedy swap) pass to each retained candidate after the main search. Cheap and almost always improves the final reach by a small margin.

arguments.name: local_improve_max_iter
arguments.description: Maximum iterations for the local improvement loop. The loop exits earlier if no swap improves the score in a full sweep.

arguments.name: wide_format
arguments.description: When `true`, emit results in wide CSV format (one column per item slot) rather than the default long format (one row per item in the subset).

arguments.name: keep_scenarios
arguments.description: When `true` (default), retain per-respondent scenario rows in the result file — useful for downstream analysis. Set to `false` for a much smaller output that contains only the aggregated reach scores.

arguments.name: keep_diagnostics
arguments.description: When `true` (default), retain diagnostic columns (search method used, screening pass counts, replicate id, etc.) in the output.

arguments.name: target_subgroup
arguments.description: Name of a single subgroup column from `subgroups` to optimise against. When set, the search maximises reach within that subgroup rather than overall reach. The other subgroups are still reported in the output for context.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of Rayon worker threads used to score candidate subsets in parallel. Applies to the `exhaustive`, `sampled`, and `beam` search strategies. `1` (default) runs serially. Results are deterministic under a given `seed` regardless of thread count.

details:
`search-k` is the workhorse single-shot search command. It picks one subset size `k` and finds the highest-reach subset of that size, with the option to include / exclude specific items and to enforce forbidden pairs.

The `auto` search method takes the guesswork out of choosing a strategy. For small problems where `C(n, k) <= exhaustive_limit`, it enumerates everything; for larger problems it falls back to sampled search with screening, which is the most reliable approach for the typical 30 – 100-item TURF problem. Beam search is an alternative non-random fallback when reproducibility across runs matters more than peak performance.

The sampled search has two phases. A *screening* loop iteratively prunes the eligible item pool using a cheap proxy metric (`screen_metric`), shrinking the pool by `drop_frac` each round until it hits `min_survivors`. The remaining items are then evaluated under the full reach calculation across `sample_replicates` independent samples drawn from `seed`. The local-improvement pass then walks each retained candidate to its nearest local optimum.

If you need to compare reach across multiple sizes (e.g., to draw a value curve), use `search-ladder` rather than calling `search-k` repeatedly — the ladder reuses anchor candidates between sizes, which is much faster.
value:
On success, `out_dir` contains:

- A top-N candidates CSV (one row per kept subset, ordered by reach).
- A single-row best-record CSV containing the highest-reach subset.
- A snapshot of the prepared input recording resolved utility columns, applied filters, and search settings.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, one `event.artifact.created` per file written, and a final `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`) with elapsed time.
notes: For multi-size comparisons (a value curve), use `search-ladder` — it's much faster than calling `search-k` once per size.
Sampled search results are reproducible only when `seed` is supplied. Without it, the RNG is freshly seeded each call.
`include` items count toward `k`. To search for a 10-item subset that must contain items 1 and 4, pass `k = 10` and `include = "1,4"` — the search then chooses 8 free items.
`forbidden_pairs` is a hard constraint; the search will not return any subset that violates it.

see_also.description: Build the full value ladder over a range of sizes — preferred when you want a curve rather than a single size.
Run anchored ladders from multiple anchor sizes in parallel.
Score a fixed list of hand-built subsets — useful for benchmarking against `search-k` results.
Sanity-check the utilities file before running a long search.

examples.title: Find the best 8-item subset with default settings
Force-include item 5, exclude item 2, with reproducible seed
examples.body:
[["maxdiff-turf::search-k"]]
utilities = "./utilities.csv"
out_dir = "./search-k-8"
k = 8
include = "5"
exclude = "2"
seed = 42

fields.name: utilities
fields.description: Path to per-respondent utilities CSV.

fields.name: out_dir
fields.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist.

fields.name: k
fields.description: Subset size to search for.

fields.name: subgroups
fields.description: Optional subgroup membership CSV (one column per subgroup).

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Optional item labels CSV (one row per item).

fields.name: include
fields.description: Comma-separated 1-based item indices to force-include in every candidate.

fields.name: exclude
fields.description: Comma-separated 1-based item indices to exclude from all candidates.

fields.name: utility_cols
fields.description: Explicit utility column spec (1-based, supports ranges like '3-12').

fields.name: non_utility_cols
fields.description: Comma-separated 1-based columns to ignore (e.g. id, weight columns).

fields.name: forbidden_pairs
fields.description: Forbidden pairs spec, e.g. '1:2,3:4'.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Whether the CSV files have header rows. Default: true.

fields.name: reach_method
fields.description: Reach scoring rule.

fields.name: number_of_alt
fields.description: Implied alternatives per task for the reach calculation. Default: 5.

fields.name: threshold
fields.description: Threshold value used by the threshold reach method. Default: 0.8.

fields.name: search_method
fields.description: Search strategy. 'auto' picks based on problem size.

fields.name: exhaustive_limit
fields.description: Maximum number of subsets enumerated by exhaustive search. Default: 100000.

fields.name: top_n
fields.description: Number of top scoring subsets to keep. Default: 100.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: Random seed (omit for non-deterministic).

fields.name: sample_n
fields.description: Sampled-search budget (overrides sample_factor when set).

fields.name: sample_factor
fields.description: Multiplier on the auto-sampled budget. Default: 20.

fields.name: sample_replicates
fields.description: Sampled-search replicates. Default: 3.

fields.name: drop_frac
fields.description: Fraction of items dropped each screening pass. Default: 0.25.

fields.name: min_survivors
fields.description: Minimum survivors retained during screening rounds.

fields.name: screen_metric
fields.description: Screening metric used during sampled search.

fields.name: top_alpha
fields.description: Top-alpha tail probability for screening. Default: 0.10.

fields.name: beam_width
fields.description: Beam search width. Default: 1000.

fields.name: local_improve
fields.description: Apply a local improvement pass to candidates. Default: true.

fields.name: local_improve_max_iter
fields.description: Max iterations for local improvement. Default: 25.

fields.name: wide_format
fields.description: Emit results in wide CSV format.

fields.name: keep_scenarios
fields.description: Retain per-respondent scenario rows in the result file. Default: true.

fields.name: keep_diagnostics
fields.description: Retain diagnostic columns. Default: true.

fields.name: target_subgroup
fields.description: Optimize against a specific subgroup column from the subgroups file.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Rayon worker threads for parallel candidate scoring. Default: 1.
